content.text: Review the sports prediction research flow and run the pull system for 4/27/26 and 4/28/26
I’ll inspect the sports prediction note and the related CLI/puller code first, then I’ll run the dated pulls for April 27 and April 28, 2026 using the repo’s existing command patterns.

The opened ledger shows the 2026-04-27 run produced zero positive-edge predictions, 43 pass rows, and several unmatched odds mostly from future-dated records. `rg` is unavailable in this shell, so I’m switching to PowerShell-native searches.

The CLI help confirms the sports research flow is split into three main pulls: ESPN slate, OddsHarvester odds cache, then `sports-predictions` to join the slate and odds into backtestable ledgers. I’m going to run those for the inclusive 2026-04-27 to 2026-04-28 window and use `--force` on predictions because dated ledgers already exist.
The ESPN slate pull completed: 18 events for April 27 and 22 for April 28. Next I’m refreshing the local odds cache, which is the only browser-scraper-dependent step in this flow.

Odds refresh is blocked because `oddsharvester` is not installed or not on PATH, so I can’t refresh the odds cache from the scraper in this environment. I’ll continue with the existing local odds cache and regenerate the prediction ledgers/notes for both dates.

Reviewed and ran the sports prediction flow for `2026-04-27` and `2026-04-28`.

Flow status:
- ESPN slate pull succeeded.
  - `2026-04-27`: 18 events
  - `2026-04-28`: 22 events
- Odds refresh did not run because `oddsharvester` is not installed/on PATH: `spawnSync oddsharvester ENOENT`.
- Prediction generation succeeded using the existing local odds cache, with `--include-pass --force`.

Updated notes:
- [2026-04-27 Sports Daily Slate](<C:/Users/CaveUser/Documents/Obsidian Vault/My_Data/05_Data_Pulls/Sports/2026-04-27_Sports_Daily_Slate_2026-04-27.md>)
- [2026-04-28 Sports Daily Slate](<C:/Users/CaveUser/Documents/Obsidian Vault/My_Data/05_Data_Pulls/Sports/2026-04-27_Sports_Daily_Slate_2026-04-28.md>)
- [2026-04-27 Sports Predictions](<C:/Users/CaveUser/Documents/Obsidian Vault/My_Data/05_Data_Pulls/Sports/2026-04-27_Sports_Predictions_2026-04-27.md>)
- [2026-04-28 Sports Predictions](<C:/Users/CaveUser/Documents/Obsidian Vault/My_Data/05_Data_Pulls/Sports/2026-04-27_Sports_Predictions_2026-04-28.md>)

Prediction result:
- `2026-04-27`: 0 prediction candidates, 43 pass rows, 23 unmatched odds
- `2026-04-28`: 0 prediction candidates, 15 pass rows, 52 unmatched odds

Main review finding: the flow itself is wired correctly, but odds quality is the weak link right now. The existing odds caches include many records whose `match_date` resolves to the adjacent date, plus a recurring bad `2017-03-09` Decin record. Since `oddsharvester` could not refresh, today’s prediction notes are based on stale/local cache rather than a fresh scrape.
Can we review the flow and workflow because the predictions and data is different each time the flow is run

I’ll trace the sports prediction path end to end and look specifically for nondeterminism: live API fields, cache overwrites, sorting/tie handling, duplicated odds rows, and any run-time defaults that change output.

The implementation confirms two separate sources can change between runs: `sports-predictions` fetches ESPN live every time, while odds are read from mutable cache files. I’m checking the cache contents and duplicate records now because the note already shows repeated F5 rows and cross-date odds records.

content.text: The cache check found concrete causes, not just live-data drift: the odds files for each date contain records from multiple dates, and the generated CSV has duplicate `prediction_id` rows. I’m going to inspect the fetch layer and tests so any workflow recommendation maps cleanly to code changes.

I’m going to make two focused changes: freeze ESPN inputs for prediction generation so reruns use the same source payload unless explicitly refreshed, and deduplicate generated rows by `prediction_id` so repeated odds records do not create changing/pass-count noise. Then I’ll update the reference workflow to make the intended run order explicit.

content.newText:
--dry-run                 Print commands without running browser scraper
  sports-predictions Generate pending prediction ledgers from ESPN slate + local odds cache
                --date <YYYY-MM-DD> | --from <date> --to <date> | --days <n>
                --sports <CSV>            Default: baseball,basketball,ice-hockey
                --min-edge <decimal>      Default: 0; use 0.01 for 1%
                --include-pass            Include below-threshold rows in CSV
                --refresh-source          Refresh frozen ESPN source snapshots before scoring
                --force                   Overwrite existing prediction CSV
                --dry-run | --json
  sports-settle Settle prediction ledgers from ESPN final scores

content.newText:
- `sports-odds` harvests local OddsHarvester JSON caches for market context.
- `sports-predictions` creates pending CSV ledgers from frozen ESPN event snapshots plus odds-cache prices.
- `sports-settle` updates prediction ledgers with final ESPN scores and fake-money tracking.
content.oldText:
- `sports-odds` harvests local OddsHarvester JSON caches for market context.
- `sports-predictions` creates pending CSV ledgers from ESPN event matches plus odds-cache prices.
- `sports-settle` updates prediction ledgers with final ESPN scores and fake-money tracking.

content.newText:
Deterministic rerun rule:
- Run `sports-odds` once to create the odds cache for the target date.
- Run `sports-predictions` once to freeze ESPN source snapshots under `scripts/.cache/sports/espn/YYYY-MM-DD/`.
- Rerunning `sports-predictions` uses those frozen ESPN snapshots by default, so the same odds cache should produce the same prediction ledger.
- Use `--refresh-source` only when you intentionally want to resnapshot ESPN data before lock time.
- Use `--force` only when intentionally replacing the existing prediction ledger.

## Prediction Ledger

- Existing ledgers are not overwritten unless `--force` is passed.
- ESPN scoreboard inputs are snapshotted for prediction generation; reruns reuse the snapshot unless `--refresh-source` is passed.
- Repeated odds records collapse to one row per `prediction_id` before the CSV is written.
- Settlement only recomputes already-settled rows when `--force` is passed.

content.text:
Reviewed the flow and tightened the unstable parts.

Root causes found:
- `sports-predictions` fetched live ESPN scoreboard data every run, so probables/status/odds exposed by ESPN could drift.
- Odds cache files are mutable and currently contain mixed-date records.
- Duplicate OddsHarvester records were producing duplicate `prediction_id` rows, especially F5 baseball rows.

Changes made:
- [sports-predictions.mjs](<C:/Users/CaveUser/Documents/Obsidian Vault/My_Data/scripts/pullers/sports-predictions.mjs:28>) now freezes ESPN prediction inputs under `scripts/.cache/sports/espn/YYYY-MM-DD/` and reuses them on reruns.
- Added `--refresh-source` when you intentionally want to resnapshot ESPN before scoring.
- Added deduping so repeated odds records collapse to one stable row per `prediction_id`.
- Updated the workflow docs in [Sports Prediction System.md](<C:/Users/CaveUser/Documents/Obsidian Vault/My_Data/04_Reference/Sports Prediction System.md:32>).
- Added a targeted test in [sports-predictions-wiring.test.mjs](<C:/Users/CaveUser/Documents/Obsidian Vault/My_Data/scripts/tests/sports-predictions-wiring.test.mjs:189>).

Validation:
- Targeted sports prediction tests passed.
- Forced rerun produced identical CSV hashes:
  - `2026-04-27_predictions.csv`: `EB94CE999F5D8966CDD676A2694224495FFC23D617E2BF6139C8E3A2701FBF22`
  - `2026-04-28_predictions.csv`: `EA3CB9F3867809788479EF120B63E666A5770155E8F6A981363E1E3D487126E6`
- April 27 prediction note now has `26` pass rows instead of `43`, with duplicate prediction IDs removed.

Remaining issue: `sports-odds` still cannot refresh because `oddsharvester` is not installed/on PATH. Until that is fixed, predictions are deterministic against the existing local odds cache, but the odds cache itself may still be stale or mixed-date.
